title: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark — Real-Time Coding at 1000 Tokens/sec (Cerebras + OpenAI)
whyTrending: Shipped May 2026 as research preview for ChatGPT Pro. ~15x faster than flagship GPT-5.3-Codex (1000 tok/s vs ~65). First OpenAI model running on Cerebras Wafer-Scale Engine 3. 128k context, text-only. Changes the loop from 'request → wait → review' to genuinely real-time edits. Zero practical workflow guides exist — only marketing posts.

title: Google Antigravity Deep Dive — Gemini 3 Pro Agentic IDE vs Claude Code & Cursor
whyTrending: Launched Nov 18, 2025 alongside Gemini 3. Google's first agent-first IDE: agents get direct access to editor + terminal + browser, plan/execute/verify end-to-end. Public preview free on Mac/Win/Linux. Gemini 3.1 Pro + 3 Flash. Most coverage is launch-day fluff — nobody has published a 'I used it for a week on a real project' breakdown vs Claude Code.

title: Jules Autonomous Coding Agent — Google's Answer to Devin and Claude Code Cloud
whyTrending: Powered by Gemini 3 Pro. Fetches your repo, clones to Cloud VM, develops a plan, executes asynchronously. Different mental model from Claude Code/Codex (local-first) — Jules is fire-and-forget cloud-native. No real comparison guides showing when Jules wins vs loses against Codex Cloud or Claude Code background mode.

title: The Multi-CLI Stack — Orchestrating Claude Code + Codex + Gemini CLI in One Workflow
whyTrending: HN-front-page tools: Mysti (debate-then-synthesize), DevSquad (delegate to Gemini/Codex when Claude context heavy), Roundtable MCP (zero-config orchestration), CloudCLI (8.2k stars). Pragmatic Engineer survey: top teams compose 3 agents, not pick one. Gap: every comparison post is 'X vs Y vs Z' — nobody writes the 'how to use all three together' playbook.

title: AI Debate Code Review — Why Asking Claude + Codex + Gemini to Argue Beats Any Single Model
whyTrending: Multiple HN posts (Feb–Apr 2026) confirm: ensemble/debate reviews catch ~30% more bugs than single-model review. Each model has distinct failure modes — Gemini superficial, Codex dependable but conservative, Claude eager but optimistic. Mysti productized this in Jan 2026. Practical 'how I wire it up locally' post is missing.

title: Codex Chrome Extension + Windows Native App — OpenAI's Push Into Browser & Desktop
whyTrending: May 2026 Codex updates: Chrome extension runs parallel agents across tabs in background, you control which sites it can access. Windows app went GA with native PowerShell sandbox — no WSL/VM needed. OpenTelemetry trace metadata added. Big DX shift but no honest user reports yet — does it actually beat Claude Code on Windows?

title: Subagents Architecture Showdown — Codex Parallel vs Claude Code Teams vs Gemini Specialists
whyTrending: Three different architectures for the same problem: Codex spawns specialized parallel agents, collects results into one response. Claude Code Agent Teams has 'team lead' coordinating teammates with own context windows. Gemini CLI subagents are 'specialists' inside main session. Each design has tradeoffs nobody has mapped out for a working dev choosing a stack.

title: Anthropic 2026 Agentic Coding Trends Report — What the Data Actually Says (Honest Breakdown)
whyTrending: Anthropic published their 2026 Agentic Coding Trends Report. Key data: 95% weekly AI usage, 75% of work AI-assisted, 56% doing 70%+ engineering with AI, Claude Code at 87.6% SWE-bench Verified. Important: it's Anthropic's data so it's biased. Honest analysis with cross-references to Pragmatic Engineer survey and Google Cloud trends report would land.

title: Gemini CLI Production Setup — MCP Servers, Skills, and the 1M Context Window in Practice
whyTrending: Open-source, free for personal use, 1M context, native tools (Search grounding, file ops, shell, web fetch). MCP + Skills support added recently. Devs report 'lightning fast' velocity but quality below Claude Opus on complex tasks. Nobody has written the production tuning guide — when Gemini CLI is right tool, when it's wrong, real config patterns.

title: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering — The Discipline Layer That Separates Shipping from Shipping Garbage
whyTrending: Dev.to thought-leader post (April 2026) reframes the era: 'models are capable now; what separates teams who ship from those generating elegant-looking garbage is discipline, not model choice.' Specs, observability, structured workflows. Matches my prior posts (Spec-Driven Dev, /ultrareview, Plan Mode) — natural follow-up that ties the whole stack together.
